title: Integrate Nitpick and optional Sledgehammer probes
summary: Add probe tasks for satisfiability, countermodels, and proof-replay hints as structured subcommands.

title: Implement premise-deletion and conclusion-perturbation harness
summary: Support adversarial checks that remove premises or perturb conclusions to expose vacuity and over-strong statements.
acceptance: Harness can generate variant jobs and summarize stable/fragile results per target theorem.

title: Expose runner subcommands: build, export, dump, audit, profile
summary: Completed: the canonical runner now exposes machine-usable `materialize`, `build`, `export`, `dump`, `audit`, and `profile` commands with JSON stdout, compatibility aliases, and documented request-file flows.
acceptance: CLI commands emit JSON to stdout and non-zero exit codes on failures; help text documents request/response files.

title: Create Isabelle integration fixtures and reproducible runner tests
summary: Completed: theorem-audit runner fixtures now cover definitional, locale-based, sorry-containing, and suspicious sessions, and reproducible CLI integration tests preserve normalized golden outputs for audit and profile runs.
acceptance: CI or gated local profile runs full runner tests against fixtures; failures preserve readable normalized diff artifacts.

title: Implement canonical repository layer over SQLite with file export mirrors
summary: Completed: engine project metadata and canonical artifacts now persist in SQLite-authoritative stores, while load/save paths regenerate JSON mirrors for artifacts, revisions, journals, and project records.
acceptance: Engine can create/load/update projects
column20: and revisions; missing mirror files are recreated from SQLite-backed records.

title: Implement claim-structuring workflow and planner admission pipeline
summary: Completed: phase1 claim structuring now runs through explicit planner admission, structuring, validation, admitted/failed states, with retry history and checkpoint errors captured in workflow DTOs before any ClaimGraph save.
acceptance: Workflow produces canonical ClaimGraph revision or structured failure; retries are logged; no unchecked JSON from agents enters storage.

title: Implement dual-formalization workflow with divergence capture
summary: Completed: phase2 now runs explicit dual-formalization workflow DTOs, persists claim-linked attempt lineage into the review journal, and preserves divergence metadata for aligned, divergent, and single-success outcomes before verification/audit.
acceptance: Workflow stores both formalizer attempts, links them to the target claim, and computes divergence metadata even when one attempt fails.

title: Implement audit workflow that composes runner outputs and deterministic rules
summary: Completed: phase2 now selects a canonical formal artifact, invokes the runner CLI audit surface, derives deterministic audit findings from trust/probe/robustness outputs, and emits AssuranceProfile without any LLM gate dependency.
acceptance: Workflow emits AssuranceProfile without requiring an LLM for gating; optional narrative rationale can be added afterward.

title: Implement promotion state machine and review checkpoints
summary: Completed: explicit promotion checkpoints now bind to the latest assurance-profile revision, reject skipped gate transitions, require override rationale above the recommended gate, and prevent the same actor from both authoring and certifying a claim.
acceptance: State machine rejects invalid transitions; review checkpoints and override rationales are recorded in the event journal.

title: Implement document-ingest adapter from claim-tracer concepts into canonical Claim Graph
summary: Completed: packages/evidence-connectors now emit source-mapping bundles with raw units, evidence items, claim candidates, relation candidates, unresolved references, provenance, and uncertainty, while services/engine alone converts those bundles into provisional ClaimGraph revisions and keeps the mapping report.
acceptance: Academic/legal/general document ingest produces a source-mapping bundle plus an engine-owned provisional ClaimGraph revision; mapping report, unresolved references, evidence items, and provenance remain available for admission review.

title: Add engine API for project, artifact, audit, and promotion operations
summary: Completed: FormalClaimEngineAPI now binds project CRUD, claim-structuring, runner-backed audit, profile recomputation, promotion approval, and export-bundle assembly behind one typed engine boundary that reuses the canonical repository and artifact store.
acceptance: Typed service layer exposes create/open project, run claim structuring, run audit, recompute profile, approve promotion, and export bundle.

title: Refactor prompts and agent adapters into pluggable provider modules
summary: Completed: agent system prompts now live in a file-backed registry, response schemas are versioned separately from prompt templates, provider-specific JSON/request shaping is isolated in adapter modules, and structuring/formalization workflow journals persist prompt hash, schema hash, and adapter version lineage for reproducibility.
acceptance: Agent code carries no system-prompt literals; provider transport does not leak into agent logic; prompt/schema/adapter lineage is reproducible from workflow journal metadata.

title: Define narrow typed MCP tool/resource facade over FormalClaimEngineAPI
prototype_basis: `services/mcp-server` already existed from the donor MCP surface, but it had to be re-scoped into an engine-owned thin facade rather than a second workflow owner.
summary: Completed: the canonical MCP server now exposes only workflow-boundary tools plus read-only resources over `FormalClaimEngineAPI`, uses job handles for long-running mutations, and keeps canonical artifact ownership inside engine.
acceptance: MCP server provides typed workflow tools for project CRUD, document ingest, claim structuring, dual formalization, audit, profile recomputation, promotion, and bundle export; resources are read-only and long-running operations return `job_id` handles.

title: Port selected `claim_tracer_mcp` ingest and query flows into canonical MCP endpoints
prototype_basis: Useful donor behaviors already existed in `claim_tracer_mcp`, but they needed to be absorbed into canonical dotted MCP endpoints without reviving legacy store/model ownership.
summary: Completed: canonical MCP endpoints now cover ingest, trace-forward, trace-backward, gap detection, assessment, graph export, and project/graph reads, while donor compatibility helpers remain outside the canonical tool registry.
acceptance: Tool names and JSON responses are documented; canonical MCP exposes `document.ingest`, `claim.trace_forward`, `claim.trace_backward`, `graph.detect_gaps`, `claim.assess`, and `graph.export`, and legacy user stories map onto the new server without reintroducing legacy models.

title: Expose proof/audit MCP workflow tools for engine-backed audit and profile recomputation
prototype_basis: Runner-backed audit and profile recomputation already existed behind engine workflows; MCP only needed to expose them as thin agent-facing control-plane calls.
summary: Completed: MCP now exposes the proof workflow agents actually need through `formalize.dual`, `audit.run`, `profile.recompute`, `promotion.transition`, and `bundle.export`, with job results and artifact refs matching the engine API boundary.
acceptance: Canonical MCP covers formalization, runner-backed audit, assurance-profile recomputation, promotion transitions, and bundle export through the engine API; parity smoke confirms MCP results match direct engine calls for the end-to-end workflow.

title: Implement structured errors, request logging, queue limits, and MCP parity smokes
prototype_basis: Expected operational controls were missing from the donor MCP server and had to be added around the new canonical facade.
summary: Completed: every canonical MCP tool now returns structured error envelopes, logs request IDs, enforces configurable queue/concurrency limits, and is covered by surface/parity smokes that exercise the full ingest-to-promotion path.
acceptance: Every tool returns machine-readable error codes; server logs request IDs; configurable queue/concurrency guard exists; MCP surface and parity smokes cover `ingest -> structure -> formalize -> audit -> promote -> export`.

title: Build human-facing CLI for project bootstrap, audit runs, and artifact inspection
prototype_basis: `apps/cli` existed only as a placeholder README; the canonical operator shell had to be created as a thin package over `FormalClaimEngineAPI`.
summary: Completed: `apps/cli` is now a Python app package that exposes project, document, claim, audit, profile, promotion, artifact, export, and legacy-import command groups over the engine boundary, with both pretty and JSON output plus optional file emission.
acceptance: CLI supports init/open/list
column25: and import commands with JSON and pretty output modes.

title: Add CLI smoke tests and scripted operator scenarios
prototype_basis: No end-to-end operator coverage existed for the new CLI surface before this wave.
summary: Completed: `tests/e2e/test_cli_operator_flows.py` now reproduces both a theorem-audit operator flow and a legal document-ingest flow through the human CLI, and root acceptance builds the CLI package and runs those scenarios.
acceptance: E2E tests cover toy theorem audit and paper/legal ingest flows; CI or local root checks can run the reduced smoke profile through the CLI package.

title: Initialize Tauri desktop shell with project browser and local process orchestration
summary: Completed: `apps/desktop` now provides a Tauri shell over the canonical CLI with project list/create/open flows, project metadata display, and local MCP service start/stop orchestration without embedding engine semantics.
acceptance: Desktop app opens local projects, starts/stops local services, and displays project metadata without manual terminal setup.

title: Implement Claim Graph and Assurance Graph navigation with React Flow
summary: Completed: the desktop shell now renders ClaimGraph navigation plus a read-only assurance projection with React Flow, target-claim focus, status/relation filters, hotspot highlighting, revision-aware diff summaries, and linked profile/audit artifact inspection over canonical engine outputs.
acceptance: User can inspect graphs by target claim, filter by relation/status, and open linked artifacts from selected nodes.

title: Implement light theory editor and managed external Isabelle launch with budgeted run control
summary: Keep the desktop as a thin shell while routing build/check/search/probe requests through managed backend runs that expose start
acceptance: poll
column20: Completed: desktop now exposes a thin theory editor, file list/read/write controls, and managed proof-job start/poll/cancel/kill over the runner governor, while MCP and CLI expose the same persisted job state without frontend-owned proof semantics.
column21: User can inspect generated `.thy`, start a managed Isabelle run, observe run state, cancel or kill runaway work, and sync changed artifact paths/fingerprints back into the app without frontend-owned proof semantics.

title: Implement Assurance Profile inspector, gate UI, and promotion workflow
summary: Completed: the desktop inspector now surfaces assurance-profile vector scores, trust/gate summaries, blockers, required actions, review events, linked audit/profile artifacts, and a promotion transition form that submits actor/role/override/rationale through the canonical CLI bridge.
acceptance: Operator can review profile vectors, inspect blockers, add rationale, and execute allowed promotions or downgrades.

title: Add artifact timeline, diff viewer, and review-event panel
summary: Completed: the desktop shell now renders artifact revision timeline entries, revision-aware diff summaries, compare-baseline selection, and claim-linked review-event inspection over canonical revision IDs.
acceptance: User can compare revisions of graph/profile/theory text and see who or what changed them and why.

title: Add bounded backend run governor and stop controls for managed proof jobs
summary: Completed: the runner now owns a disk-backed managed proof-job governor with wall/idle budgets, cancel grace windows, force-kill escalation, persisted log paths, and shared job state surfaced consistently through MCP, CLI, and desktop.
acceptance: Managed proof jobs run through a governor that supports start/poll/cancel/kill, records timeout or runaway-search reasons, and leaves no orphan worker after cancellation or forced termination.

title: Implement local document import and citation-anchor normalization
summary: Store source excerpts and spans in a normalized way so claim provenance and assurance links remain inspectable.
acceptance: Local file import preserves source_ref, excerpt, and offsets or fallback anchors; UI/API can retrieve them.

title: Implement experiment/evaluation evidence adapter
summary: Completed: structured evaluation evidence is now extracted as engine-owned revisioned artifacts, linked through claim/reference read surfaces, exposed in CLI and desktop inspectors, and replayed in the paper scenario without entering the proof gate path.
acceptance: Adapter stores experiment metadata, result summaries, and linkage to claims without entering the proof gate path.

title: Implement external reference registry and assurance-link browser
summary: Create browseable links from claims to source documents, experiments, and review notes.
acceptance: Every assurance link resolves to an artifact preview or metadata card in CLI/API/UI.

title: Add audit-rule property tests, regression corpus, and runaway-search stop cases
summary: Completed: promotion-state and governed-run regressions now cover cancel, hard-kill, idle-timeout, wall-timeout, and runaway-search behavior alongside the existing assurance-rule and promotion checks.
acceptance: Regression corpus includes vacuity, countermodel, oracle, missing-premise, cancel, hard-kill, idle-timeout, and runaway-search cases; property tests guard state-machine invariants and governed-run invariants.

title: Build full end-to-end scenarios for theorem, paper, and legal workflows
summary: Completed: theorem, paper, and legal scenario fixtures now replay ingest, structuring, dual formalization or applicable formal path, runner-backed audit, profile, promotion, export bundle, and CLI/MCP/desktop browse parity with preserved debug artifacts.
acceptance: At least three scenario suites run locally; failures preserve artifacts for debugging.

title: Write operator docs for promotion policy, review semantics, failure handling, and backend stop controls
summary: Completed: architecture and policy docs now define the managed proof-run boundary, stop-control semantics, operator response to stalled or runaway jobs, and the requirement that proof-run incidents never bypass review or promotion policy.
acceptance: Docs explain each assurance vector, each blocker, the review procedure for exceptions, and the operational meaning of cancel, kill, timeout, and forced termination.

title: Author project instructions and skill packs for engineering and architecture review
summary: Completed: shared agent-runtime contract docs, repo-local Engineering and Claude instructions, package-local operator/release skills, and lint checks now keep both agent surfaces aligned and prevent Cursor config drift.
acceptance: workflow documentation reference the same package boundaries, commands, and MCP tools; no Cursor config remains.

title: Implement release packaging, reproducible local dev environment, and smoke release checklist
summary: Completed: release packaging now emits a desktop binary, service wheels, and a release manifest; release docs and smoke checks cover reproducible local setup plus governed proof-job start/cancel/kill/cleanup safety.
acceptance: Tagged release can build desktop binaries and service wheels; release checklist includes smoke tests, known limitations, and verification that managed proof jobs can be started, cancelled, killed, and cleaned up safely.
